title: Weighted interweaved prediction
abstract: A method of video decoding includes determining a prediction block for a video block that is a weighted combination of a first intermediate prediction block and a second intermediate prediction block and generating a video block from a coded representation of the video block using the prediction block. The first intermediate prediction block is generated by partitioning the video block into a first set of sub-blocks according to a first dividing pattern. The second intermediate prediction block is generated by partitioning the video block into a second set of sub-blocks according to a second dividing pattern, wherein the first pattern and the second dividing pattern are different from each other.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method for processing video, comprising: determining a prediction block for a video block that is a weighted combination of at least a first intermediate prediction block and a second intermediate prediction block; and generating a coded representation of the video block using the prediction block; wherein the first intermediate prediction block is generated by partitioning the video block into a first set of sub-blocks according to a first dividing pattern; and wherein the second intermediate prediction block is generated by partitioning the video block into a second set of sub-blocks according to a second dividing pattern, wherein the first dividing pattern and the second dividing pattern are different from each other. 2. The method of claim 1, further including: including, in the coded representation, information about weights used for the weighted combination. 3. The method of claim 1, further including: including, in the coded representation, information about the first dividing pattern and/or the second dividing pattern. 4. A method for processing video, comprising: determining a prediction block for a video block that is a weighted combination of at least a first intermediate prediction block and a second intermediate prediction block; and generating a video block from a coded representation of the video block using the prediction block; wherein the first intermediate prediction block is generated by partitioning the video block into a first set of sub-blocks according to a first dividing pattern; and wherein the second intermediate prediction block is generated by partitioning the video block into a second set of sub-blocks according to a second dividing pattern, wherein the first dividing pattern and the second dividing pattern are different from each other. 5. The method of claim 4, further including: parsing the coded representation for information about weights used for the weighted combination. 6. The method of claim 4, further including: parsing, from the coded representation, information about the first dividing pattern and/or the second dividing pattern. 7. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first intermediate prediction block and/or the second intermediate prediction block is generated using an affine prediction method. 8. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first intermediate prediction block and/or the second intermediate prediction block is generated using an alternative temporal motion vector prediction method. 9. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first intermediate prediction block and/or the second intermediate prediction block is generated using a spatial-temporal motion vector prediction method. 10. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first intermediate prediction block and/or the second intermediate prediction block is generated using a bi-directional optical flow method. 11. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first intermediate prediction block and/or the second intermediate prediction block is generated using a frame -rate up conversion method. 12. The method of any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the weights include a first weight applied to the first intermediate prediction block and a second weight applied to the second intermediate prediction block. 13. The method of any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the weights include a first weight applied to a sample within the first intermediate prediction block and a second weight applied to a sample within the second intermediate prediction block. 14. The method of any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the weighted combination is generated from X prediction blocks, each based on one of X dividing patterns of dividing the video block into sub-blocks, where X is an integer greater than one; and wherein the weighted combination, denoted as P (x,y) for a pixel location (x,y) in the video block is: wherein Po, Pi, ..., Px-i, represent intermediate sub-blocks based prediction with the X dividing patterns, and w L (x, y) is the weighting value of P,. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein where N is an integer and Â« is bit-shift operation. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein the weighting values (w 0 (x, y), w 1 (x, y)) are (1, 3), (3, 1) or (2, 2), depending on pixel positions. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein a constant 2D array Weight[4][4] is set equal to { { 1, 2, 2, 1 }, {2, 3, 3, 2}, {2, 3, 3, 2}, { 1, 2, 2, l } }, and (w 0 (x, y), w^x. y)) is set equal to (Weight[v&3JLv&3J, 4- Weight[xS&3][yS&3]). 18. The method of claim 12, wherein values of the weights depend on positions of the corresponding intermediate prediction blocks. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein the first weight or the second weight depends on the first dividing pattern or the second dividing pattern. 20. The method of claim 12, wherein the first weight or the second weight is determined based on additional information in the coded representation. 21. The method of claim 11, the additional information includes skip/non-skip modes, or motion vector information. 22. The method of claim of 11 , wherein the first weight or the second weight is determined based on a coding algorithm associated with the video frame. 23. The method of claim 20, wherein the coding algorithm includes (1) an affine prediction method, (2) an alternative temporal motion vector prediction method, (3) a spatial-temporal motion vector prediction method, (4) a bi-directional optical flow method, or (5) a frame -rate up conversion method. 24. The method of any of claims 1 to 21, wherein the weights are included in the coded representation at (1) a sequence level, (2) a picture level, (3) a view level, (4) a slice level, (5) a Coding Tree Unit, (6) a Largest Coding Unit level, (7) a Coding Unit level, (8) a Prediction Unit level, (10) a Tree Unit level, or (11) a video region level. 25. The method of any of claims 1 to 21, wherein the coded representation includes weights in a Sequence Parameter Set (SPS), a Picture Parameter Set (PPS), a Slice Header (SH), a Coding Tree Unit, a Coding Unit, a Prediction Unit, or an initial block of a region. 26. The method of claim 14 wherein the weights w L (x, y) are inherited from a spatial or a temporal neighboring video block. 27. A video processing apparatus comprising a processor configured to implement a method recited in any one or more of claims 1 to 26. 28. A computer program product stored on a non-transitory computer readable media, the computer program product including program code for carrying out the method in any one of claims 1 to 26.
